_publications._publicationTitle: Sleep Disorders in Pediatric Patients Affected by Neurofibromatosis Type 1: Reports of a Questionnaire and an Apple Watch Sleep Assessment.

_contextSnippet: The sleep analysis performed using the Apple Watch Ultra revealed significant differences in various sleep parameters between children with neurofibromatosis type 1 (NF1) and healthy controls
details: Successfully measured multiple sleep parameters in pediatric NF1 patients vs controls: total sleep duration (7.2±0.8h vs 8.0±0.6h, p=0.001), sleep efficiency (80±5% vs 88±4%, p=0.008), REM sleep (80±12min vs 95±10min, p=0.022), deep sleep (60±10min vs 80±8min, p=0.015), light sleep (240±20min vs 270±15min, p=0.027)
